reasoning: The FedRAMP control AC-17 (4) focuses on securing remote access and privileged commands. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that no overly privileged roles (like cluster-admin) are bound to users or service accounts, which aligns with RBAC controls. Additionally, ensuring that network policies are in place to deny ingress and egress by default helps prevent unauthorized remote access. Lastly, avoiding the use of NodePort services reduces the exposure of services to the internet.
title: Remote Access | Privileged Commands and Access
